summary.en: A living museum by day and a casino-lit strip by night, this district preserves its Wild West soul within a compact, walkable gulch. The entire area is a National Historic Landmark, where 19th-century facades house modern entertainment against a backdrop of steep, forested hills.

description.en: The entire street is an attraction, featuring meticulously preserved Victorian architecture from the 1880s that houses modern casinos, saloons, and shops.
Perched on a steep hill overlooking the town, this historic cemetery is the final resting place for Wild West legends and offers panoramic views of the Deadwood gulch.
A key site within Mt. Moriah Cemetery, this is the resting place of the legendary gunslinger James Butler "Wild Bill" Hickok.
Located in Mt. Moriah Cemetery, this is the grave of Martha Jane Canary, known as Calamity Jane, buried near Wild Bill per her dying wish.
The northern terminus of the 109-mile Mickelson Trail, offering an immediate escape from the town's bustle into the natural beauty of the Black Hills.

name.en: Deadwood Trailhead/George S. Mickelson Trail Visitor Center

description.en: Located on Sherman Street, this visitor center serves as the official starting point and information hub for the famous Black Hills trail.
The official starting point of the George S. Mickelson Trail, perfect for a photo symbolizing the beginning of an adventure into the Black Hills.
Main Street is lined with numerous historic saloons, some of which have been pouring drinks for over a century, offering a direct link to the town's rowdy past.
Dozens of small casinos operate within the preserved 19th-century buildings, creating a unique blend of Wild West history and modern gaming entertainment.
The report notes that classic steakhouses are among the eateries found in Deadwood's historic storefronts, offering a taste of the Old West.
The report suggests visitors can find various coffee shops along Main Street, providing a place to pause and refuel amidst the historic atmosphere.
According to the report, casual diners are part of the culinary landscape in downtown Deadwood, housed in historic buildings and offering a variety of meals.

prompt.en: Take a selfie striking your best outlaw or sheriff pose on a historic street corner.
Find a historic building and capture a detail that looks like it's from the 1880s right next to something from the 21st century.
Find a classic saloon entrance and take a selfie as if you're about to push through the swinging doors.
Find a faded, painted advertisement on a brick wall and take an artistic photo of this 'ghost sign'.
Capture a photo that shows the glow of a modern casino sign reflecting on the facade of a 19th-century building.
Take a photo of a local food or drink that feels like it belongs in the Old West.
Look up! Take a close-up photo of an interesting piece of Victorian architectural detail on a building's second story.
Find a casino and take a creative photo of its entrance, imagining the fortunes won and lost inside.
Find a storefront that looks like it could have been here during the gold rush and capture its character.
Frame a shot of a downtown street so the steep, forested hillsides are visible in the background.
Take a low-angle shot of the street, focusing on the historic brickwork underfoot.
Find a window and capture your reflection mixed with the reflection of the historic streetscape behind you.
Take a photo of an old brick wall, focusing on the texture and age of the materials.
Capture the unique mix of mountain air and saloon history in a single photo. What does that look like to you?
Find a quiet alley or side street away from the main bustle and capture a photo of this hidden side of Deadwood.
Take a selfie with something you've 'discovered' in a local shop.
Photograph your meal and give it a creative Wild West-themed name in your caption.
If you're here in the evening, capture the moment the historic streetlights begin to glow.
Create a photo collage of 3 different interesting signs you see on Main Street.
Take a photo of a person, statue, or object that you think perfectly captures the character of Deadwood.
